title: NIST SP 800-218 — Secure Software Development Framework (SSDF v1.1)
description: Canonical practice list. Used by ilb-mappings-report to crosswalk Interlace rules against US Executive Order 14028 / FedRAMP procurement requirements. Source: https://csrc.nist.gov/Projects/ssdf

PO.1.name: Define Security Requirements for Software Development

PO.4.name: Define and Use Criteria for Software Security Checks

PO.5.name: Implement and Maintain Secure Environments for Software Development

PS.1.name: Protect All Forms of Code from Unauthorized Access and Tampering

PS.2.name: Provide a Mechanism for Verifying Software Release Integrity

PS.3.name: Archive and Protect Each Software Release

PW.1.name: Design Software to Meet Security Requirements and Mitigate Security Risks

PW.2.name: Review the Software Design to Verify Compliance

PW.3.name: Reuse Existing, Well-Secured Software when Feasible

PW.4.name: Create Source Code by Adhering to Secure Coding Practices
PW.4.note: Primary applicability — most security rules map here

PW.5.name: Configure the Compilation, Interpreter, and Build Processes to Improve Executable Security

PW.6.name: Review and/or Analyze Human-Readable Code to Identify Vulnerabilities
PW.6.note: Static analysis core. Auto-applicable to every Interlace security rule

PW.7.name: Test Executable Code to Identify Vulnerabilities

PW.8.name: Configure Software to Have Secure Settings by Default

PW.9.name: Protect Sensitive Data and Critical Functions

RV.1.name: Identify and Confirm Vulnerabilities on an Ongoing Basis

RV.3.name: Analyze Vulnerabilities to Identify Their Root Causes

defaultRulePracticeMapping._comment: Practices most security rules trivially map to. Used as a default when a rule's meta.ssdf is unset.
